title: erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum
intro:
Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum, commonly known as the annual redstem stork's bill, is a plant species belonging to the family Geraniaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine and has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The therapeutic actions of Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral activities, making it a potential treatment for various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

parts: Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions. The roots of Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum contain iridoid glycosides, including eriodictyol 7-O-glucoside, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers of Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as rutin, quercetin, and ferulic acid, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease and oxidative stress.
side_effects: Erodium malacoides f. subtrilobum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and skin irritation. Ingestion of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its potential toxicity.
